reason: The item cleanly targets admissible inference boundaries from comparative propagation and post-test electrochemistry data, especially distinguishing combustion/heat-source effects and proxy limitations of surface temperature. Decoys are largely single-fault and plausible, with the main residual ambiguity coming from option C’s thickness-vs-flame-retardancy wording (it implicitly leans on comparing different thicknesses) and mild overlap between multiple causality-overreach decoys, which slightly reduces discriminative sharpness.

The prompt supplies two concrete empirical contrasts that tightly cue the intended dual-error diagnosis (combustible barrier can worsen propagation; surface temperature is an insufficient proxy for internal electrochemical damage) and requests mechanisms plus corrective measurements, producing strong expert discrimination. Some linguistic imbalance exists because the proposal contains two claims but the barrier claim is directly contradicted by a single example (neat epoxy) which may invite overgeneralized rebuttals, and the short-answer format can admit partially correct responses that are hard to score consistently.
